candidates.why: Explicit top-line revenue label; matches total revenue/sales directly.
Also a valid top-line sales label; same values as Total Revenue and can serve as fallback if labeling changes across years.
Balancing row, not an actual revenue line.
Non-revenue operating income, not top-line sales.
Other income, not revenue.
Explicitly non-revenue.
Bottom-line profit measure, not revenue.

variables.notes: Using revenue labels as a priority list, not to be summed.

candidates.why: Best match for cost of sales/direct operating costs; it is the main direct cost bucket above EBIT.

candidates.row_label: Purchases of materials, supplies and goods
candidates.why: Component of direct costs, but incomplete because Materials and services also includes External services and inventory change.

candidates.row_label: Materials and supplies (measured at acquisition cost)
candidates.why: Balance sheet inventory line, not income statement expense.
Balancing row, not an actual expense line.

variables.notes: Selected the broader direct-cost line rather than individual components to capture full cost of sales.

candidates.why: Although an operating expense component, here it sits within Materials and services and is better treated as part of COGS/direct costs, so excluded from SG&A.
Overhead personnel cost bucket; appropriate SG&A component.
Overhead-related, but appears to be included within Employee benefit expenses, so adding it would double count.
Overhead operating expense bucket; appropriate SG&A component.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A subtotal is present. Selected best overhead buckets and excluded External services because it appears within Materials and services (COGS/direct costs). Excluded Share-based payments to avoid double counting within Employee benefit expenses.
No dedicated research and development line appears on the income statement.

candidates.why: Broad total finance cost line, but granular recurring components are available below and preferred.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses on loans recognised at amortised cost
candidates.why: Core recurring interest expense on borrowings.
Recurring financing cost related to leases.
Recurring finance cost bucket; included because no indication it is one-off or valuation-related.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses on finance lease agreement
candidates.why: Conceptually relevant but appears unused here; lease interest is already captured by the populated lease-liability row.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Hybrid Debt Instruments - Equity
candidates.why: Looks like preferred/hybrid instrument adjustment rather than recurring operating finance cost line within finance expenses; exclude.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental duplicate of interest expenses on lease liabilities; exclude to avoid double count.
Balance sheet liability, not income statement interest expense.
Balance sheet liability, not income statement interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest expenses on loans recognised at amortised cost

variables.notes: Used granular recurring finance-cost components instead of total Financial expenses to avoid double counting and to exclude any non-interest/non-recurring items not clearly recurring.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Hybrid Debt Instruments - Equity
candidates.why: Income statement item, not book equity.
Equity component only, not total book equity.
Valid total equity line, but less preferred because BE should exclude NCI when a parent-equity line exists.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent company
candidates.why: Best match for book equity excluding non-controlling interests.
Balancing row, not equity.
Balancing row, not equity.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing row, not equity.
Total balance sheet, not equity.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent company

variables.notes: Preferred parent-owner equity to avoid double counting any non-controlling interests.
No minority interest/non-controlling interest line appears on the balance sheet.
